Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class MemoryBlock64k

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.DataTypes
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Convenience class that creates a 64KB (0x10000 bytes) memory block - the full 6502 address space.

public class MemoryBlock64k : MemoryBlock, ISystemRam, IMemory
Inheritance
object
MemoryBlock
MemoryBlock64k
Implements
ISystemRam
IMemory
Inherited Members
MemoryBlock.MaxSize
MemoryBlock.Size
MemoryBlock.Read(ushort)
MemoryBlock.Write(ushort, byte)
MemoryBlock.this[ushort]
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a simple way to create a full-sized memory block without specifying the size parameter. Equivalent to new MemoryBlock(0x10000).

6502 Address Space: The 6502 processor uses 16-bit addresses (ushort), allowing access to 65,536 bytes (64KB) of memory from $0000 to $FFFF. This class creates a memory block that covers the entire addressable range.

Usage Example: 

// Create full 64KB memory - no size parameter needed
var memory = new MemoryBlock64k();

// Can address the entire 6502 range
memory[0x0000] = 0xFF; // Zero page
memory[0x0100] = 0xFF; // Stack
memory[0xFFFF] = 0xFF; // End of memory

// Size property returns 65,536
int size = memory.Size; // Returns 0x10000

Performance: Identical to MemoryBlock. This is purely a convenience wrapper with no additional overhead or functionality.

Common Use Cases:

  • CPU instruction testing with full address space
  • ROM/RAM simulation for 6502-based systems
  • Retro computer emulation (Apple II, Commodore 64, NES, etc.)
  • 6502 programming education and experimentation
  • Rapid prototyping of memory-mapped I/O systems

Constructors

MemoryBlock64k()

Convenience class that creates a 64KB (0x10000 bytes) memory block - the full 6502 address space.

public MemoryBlock64k()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a simple way to create a full-sized memory block without specifying the size parameter. Equivalent to new MemoryBlock(0x10000).

6502 Address Space: The 6502 processor uses 16-bit addresses (ushort), allowing access to 65,536 bytes (64KB) of memory from $0000 to $FFFF. This class creates a memory block that covers the entire addressable range.

Usage Example: 

// Create full 64KB memory - no size parameter needed
var memory = new MemoryBlock64k();

// Can address the entire 6502 range
memory[0x0000] = 0xFF; // Zero page
memory[0x0100] = 0xFF; // Stack
memory[0xFFFF] = 0xFF; // End of memory

// Size property returns 65,536
int size = memory.Size; // Returns 0x10000

Performance: Identical to MemoryBlock. This is purely a convenience wrapper with no additional overhead or functionality.

Common Use Cases:

  • CPU instruction testing with full address space
  • ROM/RAM simulation for 6502-based systems
  • Retro computer emulation (Apple II, Commodore 64, NES, etc.)
  • 6502 programming education and experimentation
  • Rapid prototyping of memory-mapped I/O systems
